name: merkle audit log: compliance/audit-recover.sh rebuilds the chain by replaying entries forward from the most recent valid checkpoint
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro compliance/audit-checkpoints && printf '{"seq":1,"last_line_number":100,"merkle_root":"sha256:GOOD","signature":"sha256:S","timestamp":"2026-05-13T08:00:00Z"}' > compliance/audit-checkpoints/0001.json && for i in $(seq 1 100); do bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/compliance/audit-log.sh" --append --event "e$i" --actor t --data {} >/dev/null 2>&1; done && echo "GARBAGE LINE" >> .claude-tdd-pro/audit.jsonl && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/compliance/audit-recover.sh" --truncate-to-last-checkpoint && [ "$(wc -l < .claude-tdd-pro/audit.jsonl | tr -d " ")" = "100" ] && echo ok 1>&2
